sourceSections: Course Overview and Evolution of Software Development
The New SDLC and Agentic Engineering
Impact on CS Education, Hiring, and AI Tools
Advanced AI Agent Capabilities and Architectural Patterns
Risks and Use Cases for Long-Running Agents

sections.lead: This course, now in its fourth iteration, addresses significant shifts in software development due to the widespread adoption of AI coding agents.

bullets.label: Course Details
bullets.text: The Kaggle and Google 5-day AI agents intensive course includes white papers, companion podcasts, hands-on codelabs, daily live streams, AMAs, and an optional capstone project with prizes.

bullets.label: AI Adoption
bullets.text: AI coding agents are now used by 85% of professional developers, and 41% of new code is AI-generated, making AI deployment a critical skill.

bullets.label: Course Evolution
bullets.text: The course is in its fourth iteration, highlighting the need to adapt to the profound changes occurring in the software development landscape.

sections.lead: The new Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) emphasizes

bullets.label: Vibe Coding
bullets.text: The white paper for day one introduces "vibe coding," a shift from translating syntax to expressing intent via natural language.

bullets.label: Agentic Engineering
bullets.text: Agentic engineering involves AI operating within structured, deterministic boundaries, contrasting with casual prompting.

bullets.label: Context Engineering
bullets.text: Context engineering is a modern skill that differentiates between static system instructions and dynamic agent skills.

bullets.label: SDLC Impact
bullets.text: The new SDLC collapses implementation time from weeks to minutes, making requirements specification and verification the new human bottlenecks.

bullets.label: Agent Formula
bullets.text: The formula `Agent = Model + Harness` indicates that the harness, including sandboxes and guardrails, constitutes 90% of a reliable agentic system.

sections.lead: AI-driven coding is poised to transform computer science education, shifting the focus towards high-level architectural judgment and enabling new business models.

bullets.label: CS Education Shift
bullets.text: Computer science education is expected to prioritize high-level architectural judgment over syntactic mastery, focusing on *how to think*.

bullets.label: System Building
bullets.text: This change could empower students to build entire systems or businesses during their education.

bullets.label: Google AI Studio
bullets.text: Tools like Google AI Studio are designed to facilitate this shift, moving from "prompt to prototype" towards "prompt to profitable company."

bullets.label: Creator Empowerment
bullets.text: This transformation is compared to how YouTube empowered creators, enabling individuals to build software businesses.

sections.lead: Advanced AI agents optimize complex problems using self-evolving capabilities and employ architectural patterns like sandbox environments for robust, self-improving systems.

bullets.label: Self-Evolving Agents
bullets.text: DeepMind's Alpha Evolve is an example of self-evolving AI agents that optimize algorithms for complex problems like matrix multiplication and hardware design.

bullets.label: Architectural Patterns
bullets.text: Successful patterns include agents writing code within sandbox environments and creating their own tools and sub-agents.

bullets.label: Verification Loops
bullets.text: Robust automated and human-in-the-loop verification loops generate data for agents' self-improvement over time.

bullets.label: Semantic Understanding
bullets.text: The Open Knowledge Format combined with Graph RAG architecture helps agents achieve a semantic graph-level understanding of entire systems before writing code.

sections.lead: The AI-driven SDLC brings risks like expertise erosion, but long-running autonomous agents are highly effective for specific use cases requiring continuous operation.

bullets.label: Expertise Erosion
bullets.text: Long-term risks include the erosion of human expertise with codebases and challenges in accountability for issues.

bullets.label: Human Oversight
bullets.text: Mitigation requires continuous human understanding and oversight, especially concerning security, to ensure human-driven innovation is not lost.

bullets.label: Research Agents
bullets.text: Long-running agents are effective for deep research, which involves collating comprehensive information over extended periods.

bullets.label: Dynamic Environments
bullets.text: They are also well-suited for dynamic environments like loan processing, insurance claims, and legal cases where inputs change over time.

bullets.label: Tool Optimization
bullets.text: A key challenge for long-running agents is optimizing external tools that were not originally designed for AI interaction.

sections.lead: This course prepares developers for the new AI era, emphasizing agentic engineering and practical tools while balancing opportunities with critical challenges.

bullets.label: Course Focus
bullets.text: The intensive course aims to equip developers for the new era of AI-driven software development, focusing on the shift from syntax to intent and agentic engineering.

bullets.label: Practical Tools
bullets.text: It highlights practical tools such as Antigravity and Google AI Studio to aid in this transition.

bullets.label: Opportunities
bullets.text: The discussions underscore the immense opportunities presented by AI agents for accelerated development and optimization.

bullets.label: Critical Challenges
bullets.text: Key challenges include maintaining human expertise, ensuring accountability, and developing sustainable and robust AI solutions.

bullets.label: Hands-on Learning
bullets.text: Participants gain hands-on experience through codelabs, encouraging them to deploy and share their vibe-coded applications.
